calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Beth Kramer for Lilian Rutledge (Administrator).

The Court stated that it received the declaration and questions how much of the services provided qualifies for extraordinary fees. Ms. Kramer made reference to the exhibits attached to the declaration, stated that they did tax preparation, that there were four real estate sales, that three of the properties were multi party units that were subject to rent control and that some had violations with the City. Ms. Kramer also stated that repairs were done to the car and to the house (property management), that the above mentioned services are not something most of her administrators/executors have to do, that $18,000.00 was already paid to the Administrator's Husband, that he started out a rate of $35.00 per hour, that there were 187 hours of extraordinary services which was at about $100.00 per hour and that they will adjust the payment to the Administrator if needed. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Continued To Mar-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-06-2019 Continued To Feb-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Beth Kramer for Lilian Rutledge (Administrator).

Ms. Kramer stated that the Administrator's Husband assisted with property management, readying the property for sale and income tax preparation for the Decedent, and that the Administrator did bookkeeping and ordinary services. Ms. Kramer also stated that a Friend of the Decedent was going to manage the property, but disappeared, that the Husband charged a lower fee, and that she feels these were extraordinary services because there were four pieces of property, twenty tenants, that sizemec work was done and that it goes beyond ordinary services. Ms. Kramer also stated that she thinks the Administrator would rather forego fees and wind this up, that she provided an explanation/description of the services in one of the supplemental declarations from January 18th and in the original petition that talks about the property and the husband's experience, and that she can submit a more detailed declaration along with all of the receipts. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to February 20, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
